title: The 'Pay It Next Month' Lie That Costs $8,400
hook: You've said it a hundred times — 'I'll just pay it off next month.' Here's what that promise is actually costing you.
angle: Turns credit card procrastination into concrete dollars — the interest math most people never calculate, combined with the neuroscience of temporal discounting that makes future debt feel fake.
audience: Anyone who carries a credit card balance and tells themselves they'll pay it off soon — especially 25-45 year olds who make minimum payments.
key_numbers: $8,400 total interest on a 16-year minimum payment plan
